data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize it.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **XML namespace and ToolsVersion** - `ToolsVersion="15.0"` and `xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003"` - not needed in SDK-style
2. **Import of Microsoft.Common.props** - handled automatically by SDK
3. **Configuration/Platform PropertyGroups** - defaults are already provided by SDK
4. **ProjectGuid** - not needed
5. **TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2** → becomes `TargetFramework` with `net472`


6. **FileAlignment, AutoGenerateBindingRedirects, and Deterministic** - all defaults in SDK-style projects
7. **Debug/Release configurations** - can be removed since they're already configured
8. **System references and explicit Compile includes** - SDK-style handles these automatically through globbing
9. **Microsoft.CSharp.targets import and AssemblyInfo.cs** - both replaced by SDK defaults and GenerateAssemblyInfo property
10. **Properties folder** - no longer needed

Now I'll create the modernized version and apply these changes.

data.content:
Here's my analysis of all the legacy patterns and the modernization plan:

### Legacy Patterns Found

| # | Legacy Pattern | Resolution |
|---|---------------|------------|
| 1 | `ToolsVersion="15.0"` + XML namespace | Replaced by `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| 2 | `Import Microsoft.Common.props` | Implicit in SDK-style |
| 3 | `Import Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Implicit in SDK-style |
| 4 | `ProjectGuid` | Not needed |
| 5 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | Becomes `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 6 | `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | SDK defaults |
| 7 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup`s | All values are SDK defaults — removable entirely |
| 8 | Explicit `<Compile Include="..."/>` | SDK-style auto-globs `**/*.cs` |
| 9 | `<Reference Include="System"/>` etc. | Implicit framework references |
| 10 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Replaced by `<GenerateAssemblyInfo>` + project properties |
| 11 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | SDK default for library projects |

Applying changes now:

<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
    <AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
    <FileVersion>1.0.0.0</FileVersion>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 16 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

- **Switched to SDK-style** (`Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"`) — eliminates both `Import` lines, the XML namespace, and `ToolsVersion`
- **Removed all boilerplate**: `ProjectGuid`, `OutputType` (Library is default), `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Configuration`/`Platform` defaults
- **Removed Debug/Release PropertyGroups** — every value was already an SDK default
- **Removed explicit `<Compile>` items** — SDK auto-globs all `*.cs` files
- **Removed framework `<Reference>` items** — implicit with `net472`
- **Deleted `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — assembly metadata is now generated from project properties (`Description`, `Company`, `Version`, etc.)
